digo: Achili ni nyere, chila mmwenga ana zakpwe
literal_en: Intelligence is like hair, everyone has some
idiomatic_en: Everyone has their own kind of intelligence
swahili: Akili ni kama nywele, kila mmoja ana zake
swahili_equivalent: Akili ni kama nywele, kila mmoja ana zake

literal_en: He who is full does not know him who is hungry
idiomatic_en: The satisfied cannot understand the hungry
english_equivalent: A full belly does not understand an empty one

literal_en: He who overtakes you is not your companion/friend
idiomatic_en: Those who outpace you are no longer your companions
commentary_en: can be applied to many situations, eg. financial, education

literal_en: He who is bereaved is the one who is eaten
idiomatic_en: The one who mourns is the one who must feed others
commentary_en: The bereaved person is expected to feed his guests

literal_en: He has become (like) a crocodile pulling (prey) into deep water
idiomatic_en: He lures people into deep waters like a crocodile
swahili: Amekuwa mamba anayevuta watu kina kirefu
commentary_en: Said of one who tempts others by deceitful means

literal_en: He has wiped himself on a banana tree and is going with his excrement
idiomatic_en: He tried to clean himself but the mess still shows
swahili: Amejisafisha kwa mgomba lakini bado amechafuliwa
commentary_en: i.e. He hasn’t done a good job and is still soiled. Said of one who tries to trick others but is seen through

literal_en: Savings anre not bad
idiomatic_en: Saving is never a bad idea
english_equivalent: A penny saved is a penny earned

literal_en: Savings do not rot
idiomatic_en: Savings never go to waste

digo: Akosaye rero ni mlume wa muhondo
literal_en: The one who does wrong today is the man of tomorrow
idiomatic_en: Today's wrongdoer is tomorrow's better man
swahili: Aliyekosa leo ni mtu wa kesho

literal_en: The one who slaps you teaches you how to fight
idiomatic_en: Your enemy teaches you how to fight
commentary_en: Used also in relation to insults, cunning, deceit etc. You learn by bad example
english_equivalent: What doesn't kill you makes you stronger

digo: Akuhombaye na mala, kombe zindaoga uhoko
literal_en: He who has sex using his fingers, his nails will wash in vaginal fluids
swahili: Aliyekuhomba kwa vidole, kucha zitaoga uchafu

literal_en: Men infront, men behind
idiomatic_en: True men step up whether the leader is present or not
commentary_en: When a leader is away other men must step in to solve the problem. When the leader returns he will say this proverb because he approves of what they have done in his absence.

literal_en: Children don’t carry other children on their backs
idiomatic_en: Children cannot carry each other
commentary_en: cf. The blind can’t lead the blind

literal_en: Piglets suckle each others lips
idiomatic_en: Even piglets help each other
commentary_en: People help one another

literal_en: A person who goes to hell/underworld does not return
idiomatic_en: There is no return from the grave

literal_en: A person who is high up, wait for him below
idiomatic_en: Those who are high up will eventually come down
commentary_en: Pride comes before a fall

literal_en: A person who is not shy hides his shame
idiomatic_en: A shameless person talks freely about what others would hide
swahili: Asiye na haya hujificha aibu yake
commentary_en: This person talks a lot without caring what other people think, they say things other people would be ashamed to say

digo: Aryaye jashoye kana kondo na mutu
literal_en: He who eats his own sweat has no conflict with anyone
idiomatic_en: He who earns his own living has no quarrel with anyone
swahili: Alaye jasho lake hana ugomvi na mtu

literal_en: A person who has no child does not know its bitterness
idiomatic_en: Only a parent knows the pain of raising a child
swahili: Asiye na mwana hajui uchungu wake
commentary_en: labour pains
swahili_equivalent: Asiye na mwana hajui uchungu wake

literal_en: He who has no-one of his own let him cry to God
idiomatic_en: He who has no one should cry to God
swahili: Asiye na wa kwake na amwombe Mungu

literal_en: He who doesn’t know how to groan has not yet been in pain
idiomatic_en: You don't know what hardship is until you've experienced it
commentary_en: Applied to situations such as the difficulties of being a breadwinner

literal_en: One who doesn’t know meat, don’t take him to the market
idiomatic_en: Don't take a novice where expertise is needed

literal_en: Instead of Hassan it is Hussein
idiomatic_en: One replaces another — nothing really changes

literal_en: The sea has no friend
idiomatic_en: The sea is no one's friend

literal_en: Get, eat a little, put aside a big part
idiomatic_en: Earn a little, save a lot
commentary_en: eg. when you get your salary, don’t spend it all at once, save some and make it last

literal_en: Bit-by-bit finishes chopping the log
idiomatic_en: Little by little, even a log gets chopped
english_equivalent: Slow and steady wins the race

literal_en: A free (gift) is not eaten, yesterday is not today
idiomatic_en: Yesterday's free gift doesn't guarantee today's
swahili: Bure ya jana si ya leo
commentary_en: Even if you got something for nothing yesterday, don’t expect the same today

literal_en: An empty house cannot be asked for fire
idiomatic_en: You cannot ask for fire from an empty house
commentary_en: An empty house will have nothing, no cooking will be done there, therefore no fire.

literal_en: The (food) given does not have left- overs/scrapings
idiomatic_en: When you are given food, don't ask for the scrapings too
commentary_en: If you are given what you need, you should not expect more. The cook is the one who controls the pot and decides who gets what, the leftovers belong to her.

literal_en: The poor person’s thing does not rot
idiomatic_en: The poor use everything they have immediately
commentary_en: The poor person uses what he has and doesn’t store it

literal_en: The poor person’s thing does not give birth to a calf.
The short person’s thing is eaten by the long person’s
commentary_en: A person who doesn’t plan ahead and lacks intelligence will be outwitted by a cleverer person, who can think ahead.

literal_en: The drunkard’s thing is eaten by the palm wine tapper
The weak person’s is destroyed by the wind
An empty finger is not licked
One finger does not crush a louse
The good finger is trapped in a ring
A finger does not point at a forest
commentary_en: You point at a forest when you want to farm there. Don't boast about the field you have there until you have actually cleared the field.

literal_en: Tomorrow's journey is prepared for today.
If a tackle defeats you while you are standing up you won’t be able (to win) while you are sitting either
commentary_en: If you are defeated by things while you have strength (health, money etc.) you will be even more defeated by them when your strength is gone

literal_en: Delayed you shall arrive
swahili: Chelewa chelewa, utapata mwana si wako

literal_en: The market has fallen on the snake

digo: Chibaya ni chako, chizuri ni cha mayo
literal_en: Bad things are yours, good things are your mother’s
The goat out in front breaks his horn
commentary_en: Someone who rushes into things or is always the first to speak up will end up in trouble

digo: Chichigbwirwa china uriziwe na chichirichirwa china uriziwe
literal_en: The thing that you hold onto has its own cry and the thing that you leave has its own cry
commentary_en: A child who is punished will tell you it’s not fair, but a child who is not punished will not respect you. ie. you can’t win!

digo: Chichimendzwa ni Mwanasiti, Chamkpwaya kana rakpwe
literal_en: Whatever is loved by Mwanasiti, Chamkpwaya doesn’t have a choice
commentary_en: Whatever the wife (Mwanasiti) wants, the husband (Chamkpwaya) has to buy, because he loves her.

digo: Chigumba kpwa nguluwe kpwa Mdigo chi utsungu
Chigongo cha mgayi chaangulwa ni kusi
literal_en: A poor person’s stick is destroyed by the wind
commentary_en: The wind represents the ‘big man’. For a poor person to succeed they need the patronage of the big man, otherwise they will fail.

literal_en: The new stick destroys the old one ?The old stick destroys the new one?
commentary_en: Children are negatively affected by the bad deeds of their parents. People will judge them by what their parents have done.

literal_en: The leg moves, the buttocks do not move
commentary_en: ‘dzanga’- meaning uncertain. When you sit down you don’t achieve or get anything, you have to get up and walk and make an effort.

literal_en: A pipe suits an honourable person
commentary_en: People believe that certain items should only belong to the rich and good. If a person who is known to be poor or bad suddenly has something such as a car, or a beautiful wife people will say that it is not his, or that he doesn’t deserve it

digo: Chila mmwenga ana fwerereye, gula waolagbwa ni jembe
literal_en: Everyone has their own bereavement, the frog is killed by a hoe
commentary_en: We all have a different way to die

literal_en: Every house has its sigh
commentary_en: Every house has it’s own rules and principles, ways of doing things

digo: Chilume ni kani Chilume ni kani
literal_en: Manhood is (bold and) stubborn
commentary_en: kani – very broad meaning: bold, tough, cunning, stubborn, brazen

digo: Chimanyi wa madzi wauka na madzi

literal_en: Goodness does not lack a defect

digo: Chiphala kudosa lugbwe siko kumala makonje
literal_en: If an antelope cuts a rope it does not mean the sisal is finished
commentary_en: There will be enough sisal to make a new rope. Don’t lose hope, even if things are not going well.

literal_en: A person who goes to a medicine-man does not return
commentary_en: he will have spent everything he has on him

digo: Chirima cha Beng’anya sicho cha Meng’anya Beng’anya’s hill is not like Meng’anya’s
literal_en: Beng’anya = the father of somebody,
commentary_en: Meng’anya = the mother of somebody.Your problems are not the same as my problems. A hill is steep, like problems. Me/Be are from Giryama

digo: Chiriro chiandza mwenye jirani naye akasikira
literal_en: Mourning starts at the neighbours house and he has heard it
Do not sin against a thing which has no sin
commentary_en: If you have a good relationship with someone, don’t abuse it.

literal_en: A bed is a brother
commentary_en: You are very close to your bed when you lie on it. It’s the place where you take your sorrows and illness.

literal_en: The sheep’s head listens in the little clay cooking pot
commentary_en: If the sheep had listened top advice earlier she wouldn’t have ended up in the pot.

literal_en: The thing which comes from inside you is hot gre
commentary_en: e.g. If someone wants to hurt your child (which caused you such pains to deliver) you will feel at pain

literal_en: The sign of rain is clouds
Medicine for a cricket is a cricket
commentary_en: The punishment should fit the rime. An eye for an eye etc.

literal_en: Medicine for a debt is to be paid
commentary_en: (creditor’s perspective)

literal_en: Bit by bit finishes the basket
commentary_en: If you keep on taking a little your store will soon be empty

digo: Donje kugbwa ni mlungu wa diya
literal_en: A fallen piece of dough is the God of a dog
commentary_en: The one who causes the dough to fall is the dog’s God (not the person who was eating) and the dog will thank him as he eats.

digo: Dunia ni mlomo wa diya tautsungurirwa
literal_en: The earth is the mouth of a dog, you cannot peep inside (either)
A thing is said but should not be done
An eye is to see with not to eat with
The eye of a one-eyed person
An eye does not have a curtain

digo: Dzogolo kuika sio dawa ya kucha
literal_en: The cock crowing is not medicine for the dawn
swahili: Jogoo kuika si dawa ya kucha
commentary_en: The day will always come, whether the cock crows or not
swahili_equivalent: Jogoo kuika si dawa ya kucha

digo: Fisi richirya mkpwongo, mzima funga mryango
literal_en: If a hyena eats a sick person, the well person (should) shut the door
swahili: Fisi akila mgonjwa, mzima funga mlango
commentary_en: You must take steps to ensure someone else’s problem doesn’t affect you too
swahili_equivalent: Fisi akila mgonjwa, mzima funga mlango

literal_en: The craftsman doesn’t sleep in a good place
Tying something well doesn’t hurt the hand
commentary_en: i.e. take good care of things (e.g. the money you are carrying tied in your handkerchief), it can’t hurt you.

literal_en: the ‘fungo’ that you eat is what will come out of you
commentary_en: fungo=meat or an insect. You reap what you sow, you are what you eat

literal_en: Die alone
commentary_en: A curse, said e.g. by an angry parent casting off a child, implying no further links

literal_en: A distant stick does not kill a snake

digo: Fwimbo ya mganga ni nyire na fupi
literal_en: The medicine-man’s stick is long and short
commentary_en: Medicine men often deceive in order to get money, so they will give a treatment even when they know it won’t work.

literal_en: Old things do not stink
Things to do with traditional dances are not all spoken about
commentary_en: because they are too many and some are secret. i.e. you cannot say everything there is to say about yourself

digo: Gadza-gadza ya mganga yalaza mkpwongo na tamaa
literal_en: The medicine man’s treatment brings the sick person hope
commentary_en: hope of healing

digo: Gafula ni mbii, dzogolo rachimbira ndifu
literal_en: Suddenly is ..., the cockrel ran ...
Things (fruits) which won’t fall will be shaken down (from the tree)
commentary_en: If someone won’t do something voluntarily they will be forced

literal_en: Those who don’t know what glue is get caught two by two
commentary_en: Some animals (rats) stick together when being chased and all try to go into the same hole, which makes them much easier to catch. Stupid people do likewise.

literal_en: The homestead’s log is not urinated on
commentary_en: This log is where the elders sit. You should listen to them and respect them rather than ignoring or despising them.

literal_en: A millipede and its stick/piece of rafia
commentary_en: if you throw a millipede out together with a stick the millipede will not return. If you want to make sure a problem does not happen again, make sure you take care of it properly, e.g. a person you don’t want in you house.

literal_en: The weak person’s horn is a snail shell
commentary_en: The greatest weapon of a weak person is useless, ie. he has no rights

literal_en: A mother’s buttocks buy a field
commentary_en: If a man really loves a woman there is no limit to what he will do for her and her children

literal_en: A lie travels fast
commentary_en: v. old Digo.

literal_en: The 3 days of mourning don’t happen until the appointed time
commentary_en: ie. you can’t plan a funeral until the person has died. Don’t expect things to happen without an appointment

digo: Hewa hewa ya ajema, chila mmwenga ana chiburuche
literal_en: The palm wine tappers song, every one has his own gourd
commentary_en: The song that the palm wine tappers sing means that they are doing their work. Applied to people working together and singing at the same time (ie. women pounding maize)

literal_en: Noise noise is growing

digo: Hoyo hoyo ndiyo ukala 106.Inapigbwa ela ina chitswa It
literal_en: Noise noise is the hunt is beaten but it has a head Whe be a
commentary_en: The hunters have their own signals and sounds while they are hunting n you have a discussion (piga) there must point of focus (chitswa)

literal_en: (From the) Ashes where you don’t sleep you beat away the dogs
commentary_en: You don’t sleep in the ashes but you don’t want the dogs to either. If you have something you aren’t using, why don’t you want me to use it either.

digo: Jasho ra mutu taririka wala taririwa
literal_en: A person’s sweat is not edible and is not eaten
commentary_en: When a person works he sweats and uses the money earned to buy things. He should not be cheated of those things. God will revenge.

literal_en: A person’s sweat is not eaten
Be an “insect” so that you walk with your house
commentary_en: chimehe-mehe is a type of grub which builds a cocoon and moves along with the cocoon around it. Rely entirely on yourself, don’t ask others for help.

literal_en: Be with (him) and you will know his character
A one-eyed guinea fowl scratches for a whole one
commentary_en: USed when an incompetent person tries to lead a competent one.

literal_en: Cut your nose so that you make it beautiful
commentary_en: A plea for reconciliation. If you owe a friend money, pay it back so that your friendship does not suffer. If you have argued with your parents, apologise, even if it hurts you a little to do this.

literal_en: A homestead does not return a corpse
commentary_en: When a person dies they cannot come back

literal_en: Work is not eaten by termites
commentary_en: You will benefit from your work

literal_en: One slap, a goat and a cup of alcohol
commentary_en: Something to do with celebrations

literal_en: Your brother’s war, neighbours should not enter it

digo: Kondo ya mlomo igololwa ni mlomo
literal_en: The war of the mouth is straightened by the mouth
commentary_en: A war of words can be solved by words. Disputes should not be solved through violence but through calm discussion

literal_en: Your friend’s war is killing you
commentary_en: The one who intervenes may be the one who gets killed. Don’t intervene.

digo: Koo usumbirwa wima 123.Kpwala kaoya achioya anaocha
literal_en: You take action to make your clan upright
commentary_en: If a relative is sick you go around to make sure they get the best treatment so that they will get better (upright)

literal_en: There is talking, but not listening to one another
commentary_en: People are given advice but do not act upon it

literal_en: To get lost in the forest is to know the forest
Dying is helping each other
commentary_en: When you have a problem I help you and when I have a problem you help me

digo: Kuinjira ni mara mwenga, lakini kutuluka ni pore pore
literal_en: To enter happens immediately but to exit happens slowly
To grow up is to wait for one another
Grow up then come and see
commentary_en: said to young people concerning the responsibilites and burdens of adult life

literal_en: Hurting a lot isn’t dying
A cowardly antelope has taken away his horns
commentary_en: The cowardly antelope runs away and hides when there is a fight, so he saves himself. Therefore it can be good to be a coward.

literal_en: To receive is to receive
Where there are cows there are no herders

digo: Kurima ni kushindana kuphaha ni kpwa Mlungu
literal_en: Farming is to have victory, to get is from God
(to) grow upwards, it’s hot below
commentary_en: Blessing, expression of thanks especially to a child

literal_en: To look all around furtively is to keep yourself well equipped
commentary_en: Be aware. Use all means to look after yourself, e.g. work a shamba, run a business, make savings etc. lots of small things to ensure you don’t lack provision. Also, make sure you know what is going on around you.

digo: Kusagala bure si kama kunyendeka bure
literal_en: To sit for nothing is not like walking for nothing
swahili: Kukaa bure si kama kutembea bure
swahili_equivalent: Kukaa bure si kama kutembea bure

literal_en: To be defeated by alcohol is shameful
Pretending cannot be healed
commentary_en: If you are not really sick you cannot be treated. Those who fake illness to get out of working.

digo: Kutana na indzi ni chironda kuphola
literal_en: Parting with flies means a sore is healing
commentary_en: Flies only come to an open wound

digo: Kutana na urindzi, ni kuolaga tumbiri mkare
literal_en: Parting with protection is to kill an old monkey
commentary_en: cf. killing the goose which lays the golden eggs. The old monkey is the one who was providing the protection.

digo: Kuterya, takuangamika 143.Kuvweha sana siko kulatsa T
literal_en: Helping cannot be lost/destroyed o pull a lot is not to hit Whe rele Eg. has
commentary_en: Helpful deeds will be remembered n you go hunting you pull the string to ase the arrow, but you may miss the animal. if a man chooses a woman for his wife she to agree too, it’s not his choice alone.

literal_en: Commanding is not protection
(death) is in Longo and it’s with us Digos
commentary_en: Death is everywhere

literal_en: Legs are walkers
commentary_en: If you meet someone in two places which are far apart it is because of their legs (otherwise they wouldn’t have got there)

digo: Mairi mairi Mwatunya wachimbirwa ni gari
literal_en: Two by two Mwatunya missed the bus
commentary_en: The bus will always come when you have gone away for a moment and so you miss it. You can’t do two things at once.

digo: Makalo ga simba, ganagalwa-galwa ni nyani
literal_en: The lion’s mane, the baboons roll around on it
commentary_en: Even a fierce person can be gentle

literal_en: Good things are a road/way
commentary_en: advice

literal_en: A poor person has no ears
commentary_en: everyone has ears, even a poor person (is this ironic?)

literal_en: Sorrows are taken away one by one
commentary_en: said to comfort someone who has many problems. They can be solved individually even if they appear many

literal_en: Eyes have no fence/boundary
Empty tears do not return a person sent away as a substitutionary payment for murder
Your friend’s mother is your mother too
commentary_en: You should respect her just as you respect your mother

literal_en: Funeral contributions do not rot
commentary_en: Contributions given as a sign of condolence. When you are bereaved people will bring you gifts just like you did for them

digo: Mbona unanikundza-kundza dza chingo ya mbega?
literal_en: Why do you fold me up like the skin of a colobus?
Your friend’s goat is meat
commentary_en: You don’t have any emotional attachment to it so are quite happy to eat it

literal_en: A woman is not seduced by crying
commentary_en: If a man is crying a woman won’t be impressed by him and will think him weak

digo: Mchira wa ng’ondzi unariwa ni kala 160.Mdide msena, mvyere msena A
literal_en: The sheep’s tail is eaten by a fox young friend, an elderly friend
commentary_en: The sheep’s tail contains lots of fat. A good thing is often just wasted. Eg. if a beautiful big woman is married to a small ugly man.

digo: Mdigo kataki cha myawe chivyale patsa
literal_en: A Digo person doesn’t like his companion to have twins

digo: Mfupha uchishinda fisi diya kauturya/ Mfupha uchishinda fisi diya undauweza kuphi?
literal_en: If a bone defeats a hyena a dog cannot eat it (either)
commentary_en: the hyena is stronger than the dog

literal_en: You kill Mgala but pay him his dues
commentary_en: Even if you despise those below you (eg. your employees) you must still give them what is due to them.

digo: Mgala-gala na pwa karya wari muhuphu

literal_en: A medicine man does not treat himself
The divider is still at the distribution point
commentary_en: said to comfort people who are in need. They will receive something eventually. Mganyi usually refers to God

literal_en: Towns do not hate each other
commentary_en: gonerana - old form. If they do hate each other there will be problems. People should love one another

literal_en: If a palm tappers wine is praised he usually adds water to it
commentary_en: If you are praised too much you may end up spoiling everything

digo: Mjeni aphokerwa ndata, ela matso anago mwenye
literal_en: A visitor has his staff taken, but he (still) has his eyes
commentary_en: Superficial politeness (taking a visitor’s staff inside) does not mean that other (bad) behaviour will not be noticed

literal_en: A visitor comes, the inhabitant should be healed
commentary_en: When a visitor comes good food must be prepared, which will benefit all. The visitor may have brought food too

literal_en: A visitor is rain
commentary_en: Rain doesn’t last for ever, the sun comes out again. In the same way a visitor will be with you for a while and will then go.

literal_en: A hunter doesn’t get tired, when he gets tired he roasts
commentary_en: The hunter only roasts when he has caught something. If you want something, don’t get tired and give up. Only rest when you have got what you want.

literal_en: A medicine man’s wife does not get thin A sick person has no friend W ar
commentary_en: A medicine man always gets lots of money and gifts ho will help you to carry the pain when you e sick? You have to bear it yourself

literal_en: A sick person is the granary of the medicine men
An empty hand is not licked
A long piece of rafia does not tie firewood
One who sleeps with ‘ngoyo’, when they wake up they do it.
commentary_en: If you go to bed with plans in your head you carry them out as soon as you wake up

literal_en: One who licks honey doesn’t just lick once
If a ‘bird’ gets burnt it is roasting ‘rambo’
swahili: Mnazi mmoja hauna pombe ya kutosha
commentary_en: black type of bird
swahili_equivalent: Mnazi mmoja hauna pombe ya kutosha

literal_en: Blood doesn’t run away from each other or leave each other
commentary_en: Blood is thicker than water. You will always be attached to your relatives.

digo: Mlume wangu waochera muuyu arye vidzogavye
literal_en: My husband has roasted for a baobab (so that) he can eat its mushrooms
commentary_en: The parasitic mushrooms will be destroying the tree so removing them is good for the tree, but they also make good food. Someone does a favour for someone so that they will gain also

literal_en: God sleeps alone
commentary_en: He has his own ways and thoughts which cannot be understood or known

literal_en: God does not give to you and then take from you
commentary_en: God does not take back his gifts to you

literal_en: What palm wine do you get from one palm tree?
commentary_en: One palm tree does not have much wine. You must work together

digo: Mnazi wa mchetu taujemwa na ngaro/ndaro
literal_en: A woman’s coconut palm isn’t tapped with joy
commentary_en: A woman/wife is very jealous of her own possessions. Don’t rejoice when taking/using her things. She will want a refund

digo: Mndzala kana masikiri 182b. Mndzala kana masikiro
literal_en: A hungry person has no followers A hungry person has no ears
commentary_en: Either no-one will follow a hingry person, or a hungry person has no intereszt in anything except food

literal_en: A hungry person can not bury his mother
commentary_en: When you are hungry, food is more important to you than anything else, even paying for your mother’s funeral. You won’t save up any money for the future but will eat all you have

digo: Moho tauvyala moho 195.Moyo wa mutu ni tsaka
literal_en: Fire does not give birth to fire The heart of a person is a forest
commentary_en: It gives birth to ash, it destroys

literal_en: A stupid person does not become straight
commentary_en: A stupid person is stubborn and will never change their ways or opinions

literal_en: He who walks carefully does not stumble

digo: Mrya chakpwe chitsanga kana kondo na mutu

literal_en: A person who lives a bad life is not imitated
The one whosews the clothes should try his own waist
Rejoice at a carcass, don’t rejoice at a track
commentary_en: Applied to humans. Don’t think you’ve killed your enemy until the deed is actually done

digo: Mtsimba mbira naye piya aendza atsimbirwe
literal_en: The person who digs a grave also wants to have a grave dug for him (i.e. to be decently buried)
The one who goes out to receive a visitor cannot be ignored
commentary_en: You should not ignore your host

literal_en: The breadwinner doesn’t die, the servant isn’t killed
commentary_en: He has to look after himself first so that he has energy and resources to provide for his family. If he neglects himself the whole family will suffer.

literal_en: The breadwinner is not killed nor does he die

digo: Mtsuzi wa ndenje unewa/uriwa wa moho
literal_en: The sauce of an (old) he-goat is taken/eaten when hot
commentary_en: The sauce will smell bad if not eaten straight away. An important matter has to be dealt with immediately and not left until later.

literal_en: The sweetness of tea is sugar
He who is told (something) in conversation has his own thing to say
commentary_en: If someone tells you something then you give your opinion, or own story

literal_en: (if) the trap stands up it is to rest its back b et
commentary_en: A trap is ‘bent over’ when it is set. Eg. if some of your employees leave you are freed from the duty of paying them. This is a relief if you are urdened by too many workers/wives/children c. to support.

literal_en: The one who does good is eaten by bad people
If a tree defeats a baboon, the monkey cannot (climb it either)
commentary_en: the baboon is a better climber than the monkey

literal_en: A tree falls on those who are near
commentary_en: When a problem comes, those who are closest suffer most.

literal_en: A messenger is not killed
The thief, catch him with the ear (of corn)
commentary_en: Don’t accuse someone of being a theif without proof. Catch his red-handed

literal_en: The one who sees a monitor lizard is the trapper
commentary_en: A monitor lizard is hard to spot, so only a skilled person can catch one. Similarly, the one who sees a solution to a problem is the best person to implement it or explain it to others.

literal_en: The one who lets the animal/meat rot is the one who owns the trap
commentary_en: If a trapper is lazy and doesn’t check his traps often the animal might decay. A person who doesn’t pull his weight may be dismissed from his job. If you don’t tend your crops you will not have a good harvest.

literal_en: A person can be told while he is returning, he cannot be told while he is going
commentary_en: Someone determined to do a certain thing will not listen to advice until they have run into difficulties

literal_en: A person is told that, he isn’t told this
A personn is saved by his own
A mad person is not imitated
commentary_en: Don’t follow any sort of bad example

literal_en: A baobab is covered by undergrowth
commentary_en: A ‘big-man’ needs his entourage to support him, who add to his prestige.

literal_en: The rain is waited for with a cleared plot
commentary_en: So that the crops can be planted immediately. Plan ahead.

digo: Mvumirizi agodzera rakpwe/ Mvumirizi naagodze gakpwe
literal_en: The one who perseveres waits for his own
commentary_en: If someone is in a hard situation we should be careful how we comfort them. If you tell someone not to worry and don’t take their problems seriously, you may react differently when the same thing happens to you.

literal_en: The one who destroys a country is a citizen
commentary_en: If we want development in our country we should take action ourselves and not wait for outsiders (missionaries/NGOs etc.) to come and do it for us. If we don’t do it ourselves we are destroying our country.

literal_en: You can’t sing songs about a singer
A parent does not eat to their satisfaction
commentary_en: They will put the needs of the children first.

digo: Mvyere mtsape mairo, ela kumshinda marifwa
literal_en: You can run past an old person, but you won’t beat his wisdom

digo: Mwadinda dinda mwenye utsi tauhendera mutu
literal_en: You should work hard yourself, the public won’t do anything for a person
‘Mwadziko’, the same tomorrow
commentary_en: Blessing, expression of thanks

literal_en: ‘Mwaligulu’, the matter of the owner
commentary_en: If I have something to do I should work harder than those who are helping him. e.g. if you have asked people to help in your shamba.

digo: Mwana-mbuzi aphyere-aphyere phakpwe kpwandza, ndipho akakurya-kurya kpwa myawe
literal_en: The goat kid sweeps his own compound first then eats at his frind’s place
An abnormal child cannot be hidden
If a child wets your thigh don’t cut it off
commentary_en: The problem of a child wetting your thigh does not require drastic action (amputation). Therefore forgive people who wrong you

literal_en: A well-cared for child does not lack
The child who listens is not told many (times)
commentary_en: This child obeys and only needs to be told something once

literal_en: The child who is willing to run errands does a lot of poos
commentary_en: because he will be rewarded by being given lots of food

literal_en: A child is something to borrow for
commentary_en: You use your child as a sympathy-tool when you need to ask for things/beg

literal_en: The child at the side has no guardian
commentary_en: Refers to a child who isn’t related to you and is just around. He won’t be taken care of in the same way as children you know.

digo: Mwana wa kutseka-tseka walafya ise nyama
literal_en: A laughing child refuses (to give) the father meat
commentary_en: The laughter can hide stubbornness and refusal. A clever child is not always helpful to his parents

literal_en: Your companion’s child is cold
commentary_en: You do not value another person’s child as much as your own. If you are looking after them you might not take as good care of them as you should.

literal_en: The young of a snake is a snake

digo: Mwananyau ni chidema, mwanadiya ni wa lugbwe
literal_en: A kitten is a rag, a puppy is for a rope
commentary_en: Kittens have rags tied round their necks when they are born so that they won’t be eaten by the male cats. The puppy is tied up with a rope so that it won’t misbehave. To do with bias. Some people are treated better than others.

literal_en: The beginning of a sore is a little itch
commentary_en: Some big problems start out very small

literal_en: Four by four finishes the snake

digo: Mwasaga nguluwe kazi uchiyolunga, ndivyo uhendayo
literal_en: The grinder pig, the work we have come for is what we are doing
commentary_en: Mwasaga means ‘one who grinds’. When the pig enters the field he doesn’t waste time but goes straight away to what he wants and takes it. Don’t waste time on trivialities, get on with the business in hand

literal_en: The body does not have a (preferred) side to excrete

digo: Myao achimwaga mboga nawe sima itiye mtsanga
literal_en: If your friend pours out sauce you put sand in the ugali
commentary_en: These people are quarelling and will both go without food. They both inflict pain on each other due to their dispute.

literal_en: If your friend stumbles tell him sorry

digo: Mzingirwa kamanya, ela enye midzi, anamanya
literal_en: The one who is surrounded doesn’t know, but those the town inhabitants do
commentary_en: The person affected by something is the last one to find out

literal_en: One who chases many people is not ignored

digo: Nang’a-nang’a ya kuyu, ndani ina mabulu
literal_en: Ripeness of a fig, inside it has maggots
commentary_en: Don’t judge a book by its cover.

digo: Naona makuzi kuzolana, ela simanya lume ni riphi wala chetu ni riphi
literal_en: I see ‘makuzi’ chasing each other, but I don’t know which is male and which is female
commentary_en: makuzi – type of bird. In many situations Digos are all alike, regardless of wealth or education. eg. at a wedding all the women will be dressed smartly. A good cook may be rich or poor, educated or not, you can’t tell.

literal_en: Collective oppression (on a person) cannot keep a person r
commentary_en: If a person is not liked then the group puts negative pressure on them until they leave. This can also be applied to a problem you want to get id of.

literal_en: Whom shall I eat is not the same as ‘what shall I eat’
Jealousy/envy suits a barren person
commentary_en: A barren woman will feel envy whenever she sees a child

literal_en: An elephant cannot be killed with a dry maize stalk
An elephant is not defeated by his trunk
commentary_en: The trunk is weaker than the rest of the elephant, the elephant controls the trunk, not the other way around.

literal_en: Taking ‘Ng’andu’ is going
commentary_en: Snatching something without asking permission.

digo: Ng’ombe iolagbwa ni chala cha mwenye
literal_en: A cow is killed by its owner’s finger
commentary_en: The owner has the power to decide which cow to kill and can just point at it and everyone will know. This applies to anyone with power.

literal_en: An angry cow has its own person to milk it
commentary_en: A person who is always angry will have someone who knows how to calm them and get things from them.

digo: Ng’ondzi kuuya nyuma, siko kuchimbira kondo, ela ni kuaza mizungu
literal_en: (When) a sheep takes some steps backwards, it isn’t running from a war, but is thinking about attacking
commentary_en: Don’t be fooled if you see your enemy/problem retreating a little, it may come back with greater force

literal_en: A sheep looks behind/backwards
commentary_en: A clever person is aware of the past and takes it into account

literal_en: A judge is a foot
commentary_en: Traditional judges try cases and receive a reward. They will only be rewarded if they actually make the effort to go and try the case (using their feet). ie. you have to put something in before you will get something out.

literal_en: The strength of water is stones
Two paths/ways defeat a hyena

digo: Nndaryani si sawa na nndarya nani
literal_en: ‘What will I eat?’ is not the same as ‘what will I eat with it?’
commentary_en: In the first instance you have nothing, in the second you have something but it is not complete

digo: Nyani kuhenda ngoti nkukala diya rikure 278.Nyasi zina mimba, gomba kusaza/uchisaza
literal_en: A baboon kneeling usually the dog is far away The grasses are pregnant, don’t say everything
commentary_en: The dog will attack the baboon if it is kneeling. Children with a strict father will do what they like when he is not around! When you are out in the open you may be overheard if you speak about secret things as there may be people hiding in the grass.

literal_en: The grasses are pregnant
‘a niggle’ is an illness
commentary_en: You have an illness (eg. aches and pains) but it doesn’t stop you from doing your everyday business. It may kill you one day though

literal_en: (when a) snake has died a red-tailed lizard bites people
commentary_en: Furious/strong people have gone, now the weak ones are flexing their muscles

literal_en: (in) a house where someone has died you should not listen to its mourners
commentary_en: Don’t believe all the stories people will say when someone has died, ie. that the person did not die naturally

literal_en: A house is problems, it was made out of many parts
commentary_en: Each house has its own problems

literal_en: A house is four corners
commentary_en: Digo houses are usually square rather than round

digo: Nyumba ya gbwalala i lala gbwalala
literal_en: (only) A beast sleeps in a beast’s house
commentary_en: gbwalala – a kind of animal, but no-one knows what it is, also refers to a person who is a beast.

literal_en: (in a) House of a bachelor there is no mourning
commentary_en: He is the only one living there, so if he dies there will be no-one there to mourn him, the funeral will take place elsewhere. A warning that people should not live alone. Having a good funeral is v. important in Digo culture.

literal_en: A weak spirit is danced for at the side
commentary_en: No one takes a weak spirit seriously

literal_en: Money is (like) nakedness, they are not shown/exposed
commentary_en: You don’t tell other people how much money you have.

literal_en: The place where you harvest is the place where you eat.
Where there is smoke there is no lack of ‘birds’ When the bush is burning this bird gathers to eat
commentary_en: the insects driven up by the smoke.

literal_en: Where there is a vulture there is a carcass
Where it is slippery do not make a boast
commentary_en: If you boast you may fall at any moment

literal_en: Where there is no hill do not make a hill (Act
commentary_en: You cannot create something out of nothing. ual meaning of chidzinyo?)

literal_en: A place with elders is not disturbed by things
commentary_en: Nothing will go wrong while there are elders around. They can sort out any problem

literal_en: Where there is smoke there doesn’t lack fire

digo: Rero ni matso machero ni mabatso
literal_en: Today is eyes, tomorrow is bigger eyes
commentary_en: You are being warned today and if you don’t heed the warning tomorrow you will face the consequences and many people will be focussing on you

literal_en: If it (a millepede) dies well it looks like the rain
commentary_en: If a millepede dies without being crushed its body produces rings which look a bit like rain. When an argument has been settled there is no need to worry again about this issue

literal_en: The soul tricked its master

digo: Safari ya muhondo i funganywa rero
literal_en: The journey of tomorrow is packed for today
A dead ear cannot feel (lit: hear) medicine
The ear does not surpass the head
commentary_en: The ear is smaller than the head, and refers to a child-father relationship

literal_en: The lion who walks quietly is the one who eats the meat.
swahili: Simba mwenda kimya ndiye mla nyama
swahili_equivalent: Simba mwenda kimya ndiye mla nyama

digo: Singo ya kuku i vundzirwa kanda

literal_en: There is no medicine for character
commentary_en: Aleopard cannot change its spots

literal_en: You don’t get a small person who does not belong to a clan.
commentary_en: Everyone belongs to a clan and therefore has a network. No-one stands alone.

literal_en: There is no market which has no appointment
commentary_en: Everything has to be planned

digo: Takuna kanga wa kare na moho
literal_en: You don’t get an old guinea fowl and fire
commentary_en: Any guinea fowl that wanders too near the fire will end up in the cooking pot – old or young. An older person is just as capable of making mistakes as a younger person.

literal_en: There is no joking about the throat
commentary_en: If someone goes for your throat you know they are serious about harming you and not fooling around

literal_en: No one gets old on a journey
commentary_en: God will provide while you are travelling

literal_en: There is no person who is so tall he sleeps in two beds
commentary_en: Even a very tall person only has one bed to sleep in.

literal_en: Things cannot be pounded if the pestles are not upright
commentary_en: When there is no maize to pound the pestles are left lying on the ground. E.g. You slander someone and say untrue things about them to their superiors. If the slandered person then goes on to do the things you have unjustly accused them of, you will feel vindicated and will report them with greater confidence. By your earlier accusations you had ‘lifted the pestle upright.’

literal_en: There is nothing inside, there is nothing inside means that they are inside
Abundance cannot be peeped into
The contribution of a Digo person is slander
commentary_en: Many Digos go around slandering each other for their own gain

digo: Tsukizi za mchizi ni raha ya mvuvi
literal_en: The cuttle fish's anger is the fisherman's delight
Bad tobacco begins at the stem
If you hide an illness you will be spoken of in mourning
commentary_en: A hidden problem will get so bad you can no longer hide it, especially if you don’t seek help or take action.

digo: Uchihega kanga na kpwiru undarya manga mhuphu
Uchihega na mwerevu mriche akaondze iye

literal_en: If you lose out at the boma/cattleshed, wait for it in the pot
commentary_en: If you find the cow you want to buy is too expensive, don’t lose hope. It will eventually be slaughtered and you may get to eat some of the meat of that very same cow one way or another.

literal_en: If you follow the dance then dance
commentary_en: Do the thing you have come to do and don’t be distracted by other things.

literal_en: If you know this your friend knows that
When you go hunting don’t chose an animal
commentary_en: You kill whatever comes before you

digo: Uchirya kuku wa myao marondo galola kpwako
Uchirya na chipofu, usimgbwire mkpwono 332.Uchirya, rya cha tele, cha sinda saza f
literal_en: When you eat with a blind person, do not hold their hand When you eat, eat until you are full, save some P or tomorrow
commentary_en: lan ahead

literal_en: If you want something from underneath something you must be prepared to bend down
If you laugh at the first signs of leprosy you will become a leper
commentary_en: Take the early signs of illness seriously

literal_en: The greatness of the ‘bird’ comes and goes
commentary_en: mlamba – small black bird

literal_en: The bow is tight, the string is tight
commentary_en: Therefore you will be able to shoot the arrow well.

literal_en: Foolishness gives birth, cunning does not give birth
You were deceived by the darkness, you urinated in the compound
commentary_en: You did a stupid thing thinking that no-one would find out, but people saw you, so don’t think you can get away with it!

literal_en: You had sex in the fire and you burnt your pubic hair
The noisy one does not kill
commentary_en: If you make a lot of noise while you are hunting the animals will all run away.

literal_en: The stubborn one does not kill
commentary_en: If this person doesn’t work with the team they will not succeed in the hunt.

literal_en: The one with a snake is morning
commentary_en: If you want to kill a snake do it early in the morning before the sun falls on it. Snakes come out early in the morning to catch the sun before it is too hot and they go and hide. If you want to do something, the earlier the better.

literal_en: Sickness increases at night
The truth of a child is irritation
commentary_en: Children never have anything important to say

digo: Undatsapa na phaphi na madiya ga Tiwi?
literal_en: How will you pass the place where the Tiwi dogs are
commentary_en: The Tiwi dogs are many and fierce. Many interpretations incl. You can’t pass through a place with tight security. There is nothing you can do to please all people.

literal_en: Much is a pestle
commentary_en: If you can’t beat them join them

digo: Usibere ngorowa, mana ngorowa ndiyo ngoma
literal_en: Don't despise the ngorowa dance because it is (a true) dance
commentary_en: Don't despise your parents whatever their stature/nature because they are your own.

literal_en: Don't buy dry cassava and dance
commentary_en: Buy carefully, then cook the cassava, only then will you know that it is good and you can rejoice.

digo: Usiguze ga kaphuni na kuno/mbavi ga mafungo bado
literal_en: Don’t sell from the basket while you still have your heaps (of vegetables)
commentary_en: Deal with the matter in hand first of all, then take care of other things

literal_en: Don’t be like the one who asks for a sheep
commentary_en: … and doesn’t ask for it straight away. In other words go straight to the point or otherwise you might miss the boat!

literal_en: Clothes are not sewn in the dark of the night
Don’t despise a bad smell, if you grill it it’s food (stew)
commentary_en: The smell is made by the ‘fungo’ (civet cat) which leaves its trace on a tree. However, if you kill the animal you can eat it.

literal_en: Do not play around with skin complaints, when they burst they become leprosy

digo: Utsapapho pore ndipho wedzapho ukaphatwapa mairo
literal_en: The place you pass slowly is the place where in the future you may be running past
commentary_en: When you want something from a person you approach them in a very friendly manner, but when it comes to paying back you run away from them.

literal_en: If you laugh at a skin disease you will get leprosy
commentary_en: Don’t make fun of other people’s misfortunes as something worse may happen to you

literal_en: The public is (like) a pestle, it cannot be folded You cannot manipulate the public

digo: Utsungu wa mwana umanywa ni mvyazi
literal_en: The bitterness/pain of the child is known by the parent (one who gave birth)
How you are born is not how you become old
Old age is a rubbish tip
commentary_en: Both good and bad things are dumped on you

literal_en: Old age is tiring
Chase away those who are eating the maize
commentary_en: Refers to baboons in your maize. Chase them first before turning your attention to those who are still in the trees. Concentrate on the main matter in hand without being distracted by side issues.

literal_en: Good eats the fruit tree
commentary_en: Mnago is a type of fruit tree. The tree which is bearing fruit is destroyed by someone breaking off the branches to get the fruit. Refers to the good one has done, but I’m still unclear as to the relationship here!

digo: Vinono vinatsupa na rome ra achiya
literal_en: Good things pass through the poor people’s teaching place
Pull pull brings a cut
commentary_en: Two people pulling in opposite directions will break the rope

digo: Vwindi kalamusa goyo na goyo kalamusa vwindi
literal_en: The front of the knee does not greet the back of the knee, and the back of the knee does not greet the front of the knee
the naughty child is your naughty child
commentary_en: Of the children you are going to bear, some will not be as you expect. However, even if he is naughty sometimes, at other times he will be good and anyway he is still your child whatever.

literal_en: A person with an eye is not shone upon
The mad person is not chased naked
commentary_en: If a madman is running around naked make sure you are fully dressed before you chase him. Don’t do as he does.

literal_en: The mad person is yours
The one with a great desire does not ripen
commentary_en: If you desire fruit so much you will pick it before it is ready, or you won’t cook your meat properly

literal_en: A stinking person does not know (it) himself
commentary_en: Applies to many things such as pride, foolishness

literal_en: The lustful one is never told to bend over
commentary_en: ie. they never need to be told!

literal_en: The toothless one cannot not light a fire
commentary_en: He cannot blow enough to light it

literal_en: He has has one thing cannot be robbed of two things

digo: Wakala kanga wa tsongo achifukurira azima
literal_en: You were (like) a one-eyed guineafowl scratching for the (other) sighted ones
commentary_en: The blind guineafowl cannot see all of the things he is scratching up so the other birds will come and take advantage of this.

literal_en: You ate ‘bombo’, you’ll pay for ‘bombo’
commentary_en: Meaning of ‘bombo’ uncertain. If you misuse something you will have to pay for it

literal_en: A bad song is not used to soothe a baby

digo: Wivu n'chironda uchishiriki undaonda 384.Yezira chakpwe ni ndezi Whoeve
literal_en: Jealousy is a sore, if you share in it you will waste away r disowns his own is a wild rat When leg i This thems a sho
commentary_en: the rat is caught in a trap it gnaws off its n order to escape, thus disowning the leg. is the same with someone who cuts elves off from their family (even if just for rt time out of pique)

digo: Yuya ni mmwenga, hara ni anji
literal_en: That is one (person), these are many
commentary_en: If you just have one child, people will ask questions, because in the future you may need help and this is better supplied by many children rather than just one. Justification for large families.

literal_en: ‘It is burning! It is burning!’ means it is nearly cooked
